data.description:
# Release build fails: check_build_env_locked doesn't PEP 503-normalize package names

The hash-locked `--no-isolation` release build aborts because
`scripts/check_build_env_locked.py` compares package names WITHOUT PEP 503
canonicalization, so a package whose `pip freeze` name uses `.`/`_` but whose lock
entry uses `-` is falsely reported "installed but ABSENT from the lock", failing the
build job and blocking every release.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Dispatch `release.yml` from `main` (v0.8.0 attempt, run 29467136412).
2. The `Build distributions (build-once)` job runs
   `python scripts/check_build_env_locked.py --lock .github/release-requirements.txt --freeze <(pip freeze)`.
3. It exits 1 with: `jaraco.classes==3.4.0: installed but ABSENT from the lock` (also
   `jaraco.context`, `jaraco.functools`, `pyproject_hooks`, `readme_renderer`).

Local repro:
`python3 -c "import sys;sys.path.insert(0,'scripts');import check_build_env_locked as m;print(set(m._parse_pins('jaraco-classes==3.4.0'))==set(m._parse_pins('jaraco.classes==3.4.0')))"`
prints `False`.

## Expected vs Actual

- **Expected:** `jaraco.classes` (from `pip freeze`) and `jaraco-classes` (in the
  pip-compile lock) are recognized as the SAME package (PEP 503: runs of `[-_.]`
  collapse to a single `-`), so the subset check passes and the build proceeds.
- **Actual:** the two spellings hash to different keys, so the installed package is
  judged absent from the lock and the build FAILS — even though it is pinned.

## Root cause

`_parse_pins` lowercases the name and strips an `[extra]` suffix but does not apply PEP
503 name normalization. `build`/`twine`'s transitive deps (`jaraco-classes/-context/
-functools`, `readme-renderer`, `pyproject-hooks`) are pinned in the lock with hyphens
but reported by `pip freeze` with dots/underscores, so they never match. This is the
first release through the new pinned build-once pipeline (08a8/6168 landed after v0.7.1),
so the mismatch was never exercised.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `scripts/check_build_env_locked.py` canonicalizes every package name with PEP 503
  normalization (`re.sub(r"[-_.]+", "-", name).lower()`) on BOTH the lock and freeze
  sides before comparing (verify: the two spellings map to one key —
  `python3 -c "import sys;sys.path.insert(0,'scripts');import check_build_env_locked as m;assert set(m._parse_pins('jaraco-classes==3.4.0'))==set(m._parse_pins('jaraco.classes==3.4.0'))"`).
- [ ] A regression test in `tests/unit/test_check_build_env_locked.py` fails before the
  fix and passes after: a lock with `jaraco-classes==3.4.0` and a freeze with
  `jaraco.classes==3.4.0` (and an `_`/`.` variant) is accepted as lock-consistent
  (`main([...]) == 0`), and a genuine version mismatch on a normalized name still fails.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass, and re-dispatching `release.yml`
  from `main` with `version=0.8.0` gets past the build's env-lock guard.
data.title: Release build fails: check_build_env_locked missing PEP 503 name normalization
